Title: Regulatory Uncertainty
Description: Fintech-specific laws are still evolving. Decree 94 (sandbox) only covers 3 activities. Crypto framework still in pilot phase. Rules can change with limited notice.

Title: Licensing Complexity
Description: Multiple regulators (SBV, SSC, MOF, MIC) with overlapping jurisdictions. License processing can take 3-12 months. Requirements may change during application.

Title: Data Localization
Description: Cybersecurity Law requires certain data to be stored in Vietnam. Cross-border data transfer requires government approval in some cases.

Title: AML/CFT Compliance
Description: Vietnam is on FATF grey list monitoring. Enhanced compliance requirements for financial institutions. KYC/AML standards are tightening.

Title: Currency Risk (VND)
Description: VND has depreciated ~3-5% annually vs USD over the past decade. SBV manages the exchange rate within a band. Capital controls exist on large transactions.

Title: Competition Intensity
Description: E-wallet market has 40+ players — consolidation expected. Banks are launching their own fintech arms. Regional giants (Grab, Sea) have deep pockets.

Title: Consumer Trust
Description: Several P2P lending platforms have failed, damaging sector reputation. Crypto scams created public skepticism. Trust building takes time.

Title: Talent Shortage
Description: Demand for fintech talent exceeds supply. Senior product, compliance, and risk management roles are hard to fill. Salary inflation in tech sector.

Title: Infrastructure Gaps
Description: Internet reliability varies outside major cities. Payment acceptance in rural areas is limited. Data center capacity is growing but still behind regional leaders.

Title: IP Protection
Description: IP enforcement is improving but still weaker than developed markets. Software patents are difficult to enforce. Trade secret protection requires careful contracts.

Title: Bureaucracy
Description: Government processes can be slow and require multiple touchpoints. Relationships matter — having a local partner or advisor is strongly recommended.

Title: Tax Disputes
Description: Transfer pricing audits are increasing. Tax authorities may interpret regulations differently than expected. Pre-ruling options are limited.

Title: Political Stability
Description: One-party system provides policy continuity. Anti-corruption campaigns can disrupt business relationships. Leadership transitions are managed internally.

Title: Geopolitical Tensions
Description: South China Sea disputes create periodic tensions. Vietnam maintains balanced foreign policy (US, China, ASEAN). Trade diversification reduces concentration risk.

Title: Climate & Natural Disasters
Description: Central Vietnam (including Da Nang) is exposed to typhoons (Sept-Nov). Flooding in Mekong Delta affects agriculture. Climate adaptation infrastructure is improving.

Title: Global Economic Slowdown
Description: Export-dependent economy is exposed to global demand. FDI flows can slow during global recessions. However, Vietnam has historically recovered faster than peers.
